what_it_does: It is a complete, descriptive inventory of 15 small registry or type directories in the company project, listing each directorys purpose, entry count, self-registration pattern, schema, consumer, and notable facts.
observations: Documents 13 row-discovered registries plus the introspection engine and the ui-contract spec directory.
Reports 128 total small-registry entries, read from 130+ files as of 2026-06-28.
All row registries use a unified file-per-entry mechanism: adding a row means dropping a file, with zero engine code edits.
Row files are pure data (imports plus a module-level dict); no def or class, except introspection engine code and platforms/_wiring.py bootstrap.
Each registry validates entries fail-loud at discovery, and the file stem must equal the declared id.
mode_detection_rules is the only registry where entry priority drives first-match-wins ordering.
board_edges reuses the RelationTypeRegistry class from relation_types but lives in a separate directory for structural/provenance edges.
decisions have no state column; decided state resolves from the latest decision_take mark on the address.
decision subtype is mandatory in practice but not enforced by the schema, and missing subtype makes a decision invisible on the operator surface.
All flows are enforced as proposes_only: True and never resolve, approve, or dispatch.
introspection carries zero platform-name literals and has been proven with three CLI families.
ai_tics are catalogued from named operator frustrations and are extensible by adding files.
Many declarative-direct authoring seams are explicitly flagged UNBUILT.
standouts: Registry-is-truth: add-a-row equals drop-a-file across 13 registries with one discovery mechanism.
Decisions are stateless rows; resolution comes entirely from append-only marks on the decision address.
The Mirror-Registry engine is platform-agnostic and has generalized to three distinct CLI tool families without engine edits.
board_edges and relation_types share one mechanism but deliberately keep separate vocabularies.
conventions: File-per-entry self-registration with a module-level dict named after the registry type.
File stem must equal the dict id for addressable-by-file discipline.
Pure-data row files; executable bindings live in engine modules or a _wiring bootstrap file.
Fail-loud validation at registry discovery time.
Cross-registry mechanism reuse with separate directories for separate vocabularies.
Decision state is resolved from append-only marks rather than stored on the row.
concerns: decision subtype is mandatory in practice but not enforced by the schema, risking silently invisible decisions.
The inventory table includes an item_types duplicate row, which may confuse counts.
ui-contract and introspection are included in a small-registry inventory despite not being row-discovered registries, blurring the category.
Attachment_types is described as not a classic row-discovered registry yet is listed alongside row registries.
A large number of create_* authoring seams are flagged UNBUILT, indicating the authoring surface is incomplete.
The 130+ files_read claim is not itemized in this file.
notes: A reference map of the companys declarative, file-discovered small registries, emphasizing drop-a-file extensibility, pure-data discipline, and load-bearing validation.
questions: Which exact files make up the 130+ files_read and how was the 2026-06-28 read performed?
What is the precise validation code for each registrys _build_* gate?
How are priority ties or collisions handled in mode_detection_rules?
What is the full relationship between decisions/ and decision_subtypes/ beyond subtype lookup?
How does the resolver consume axes fields and value_source in practice?
What is the planned implementation order for the flagged UNBUILT create_* authoring seams?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file explicitly calls itself a complete descriptive inventory of small registry directories and delivers directory-by-directory details, counts, mechanisms, and notable facts.
apparent_themes: Registry-is-truth and declarative data as code.
intention_signals: To make the small-registry layer legible as a single coherent substrate where extending vocabulary or behavior is done by adding data files rather than editing engine code, while surfacing boundaries, seams, and remaining unbuilt authoring tools.
novelty: The systematic treatment of 13 distinct vocabularies as one unified file-discovered registry pattern, plus the explicit rule that decisions have no state column and resolve entirely from append-only marks on their address.
contribution: A consolidated, neutral map of all small registries, their schemas, consumers, and cross-cutting rules, which a fused system could keep as living documentation of how declarative rows are discovered, validated, and wired.
summary_for_embedding: area-G-registries.md is a complete inventory of 15 small registry or type directories in the company project, documenting file-per-entry self-registration patterns, schemas, consumers, and notable facts for 128 row-discovered entries plus the introspection engine and UI contract spec.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to make the declarative registry layer legible and to record the drop-a-file extensibility, pure-data discipline, validation rules, and open authoring seams of the companys small registries.
